title: vibeColors - Project Context
text: vibeColors R package provides collection color palettes designed use ggplot2. package offers discrete continuous color scales integrate seamlessly ggplot2 plotting system.

title: vibeColors - Project Context
text: Multiple predefined color palettes (CBsafe1, Crimson, Plum, Azure, Turquoise, Emerald, Sunshine, Tangerine, AzureCrimson, AzurePlum, Avedon) Discrete color scales categorical data (scale_color_vibe(), scale_fill_vibe()) Continuous color scales continuous data (scale_color_vibe_c(), scale_fill_vibe_c()) Palette reversal interpolation capabilities Full ggplot2 integration

title: vibeColors Package Guidelines
text: Naming: Use snake_case functions variables Scale naming: Follow pattern scale_{color|fill}_vibe[_c] _c suffix indicates continuous scales Reference parameters/functions using backticks: `name` Include call. = FALSE error/warning messages Add placeholder examples comments features incomplete Palette definitions: Add inline comments color names next hex codes (see R/palettes.R)

title: vibeColors Package Guidelines
text: Dual-purpose design: package serves two use cases: 1. ggplot2 integration: Scale functions seamless plotting 2. Direct palette access: Users can retrieve palettes vectors via vibe_palette() custom use Palette system: - palettes defined vibe_palettes list R/palettes.R - Never access vibe_palettes directly—always use vibe_palette() function - vibe_palette(name, n, reverse) handles validation, interpolation, reversal - Returns character vector hex codes internal use user-facing API Scale integration ggplot2: - Discrete scales: Use ggplot2::discrete_scale() palette function - Warn interpolate n > length(pal) - See R/scales.R pattern - Continuous scales: Use ggplot2::scale_{color|fill}_gradientn(colors = pal) - scales accept reverse parameter ... ggplot2 passthrough - Scales internally call vibe_palette() retrieve colors

title: vibeColors Package Guidelines
text: Dependencies: - Imports: ggplot2 (>= 3.0.0), scales - Suggests: testthat (>= 3.0.0), knitr, rmarkdown
code: devtools::document()     # Generate roxygen docs (NAMESPACE, man/) devtools::test()        # Run testthat suite devtools::check()       # Full R CMD check devtools::install()     # Install package locally

title: vibeColors Package Guidelines
text: Use skip_if(length(list_vibe_palettes()) == 0) tests require palettes (tests/testthat/test-palettes.R) Validate hex format expect_true((grepl("^#", pal))) Test error messages non-existent palettes

title: vibeColors Package Guidelines
text: R/palettes.R: Palette data + vibe_palette() + list_vibe_palettes() R/scales.R: scale functions (discrete/continuous, color/fill) R/vibeColors-package.R: Package-level documentation tests/testthat/: testthat unit tests Adding features: - New palette → Add vibe_palettes list palettes.R - New scale → Add scales.R following existing 4 functions templates

title: vibeColors
text: [!Note] README drafted AI verified human authors. Please report issues. collection aesthetically pleasing color palettes designed data visualization ggplot2. Provides discrete continuous color scales integrate seamlessly ggplot2 plotting system, along utility functions working colors.

title: vibeColors
code: # View all available palettes list_vibe_palettes()

title: vibeColors
code: # View all palettes view_vibe_palettes()  # View specific palettes view_vibe_palettes(c("Crimson", "Azure", "Avedon"))

title: vibeColors
text: Use discrete scales mapping categorical variables:
code: # Discrete fill scale ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), fill = factor(cyl))) +   geom_bar() +   scale_fill_vibe("Avedon") +   labs(title = "Cars by Cylinder", fill = "Cylinders")  # Discrete color scale for scatter plots ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(am))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_vibe("Benedict") +   labs(title = "Weight vs MPG", color = "Transmission")

title: vibeColors
text: Use continuous scales mapping numeric variables:
code: # Continuous color scale ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = hp)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_vibe_c("Azure") +   labs(title = "Weight vs MPG", color = "Horsepower")  # Continuous fill scale for heatmaps ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_vibe_c("Turquoise") +   labs(title = "Old Faithful Eruptions")

title: vibeColors
text: scale functions support additional customization:
code: # Reverse palette order scale_fill_vibe("Crimson", reverse = TRUE)  # Adjust saturation (0 = grayscale, 1 = original, 2 = more saturated) scale_color_vibe_c("Azure", saturation = 0.5)  # desaturated scale_color_vibe_c("Azure", saturation = 1.5)   # more vibrant

title: vibeColors
text: can extract specific colors palettes:
code: # Get a specific color (palette + weight) vibe_color("Azure500") vibe_color("Crimson100")  # Get multiple colors vibe_color(c("Azure100", "Azure500", "Azure700"))

title: vibeColors
code: # Get all colors from a palette vibe_palette("Avedon")  # Get a specific number of colors (interpolated if needed) vibe_palette("Azure", n = 5)  # Reverse the palette vibe_palette("Crimson", reverse = TRUE)

what: Discrete Color Scale for Vibe Palettes — scale_color_vibe

text: Apply vibe color palette discrete data ggplot2.

title: Discrete Color Scale for Vibe Palettes — scale_color_vibe
code: scale_color_vibe(palette, reverse = FALSE, saturation = 1, ...)

title: Discrete Color Scale for Vibe Palettes — scale_color_vibe
text: palette name palette use. Use list_vibe_palettes() see available options. reverse Logical. palette reversed? Default FALSE. saturation Numeric value 0 2 adjust color saturation. Values less 1 desaturate colors (0 = grayscale), values greater 1 increase saturation. Default 1 (adjustment). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale().

title: Discrete Color Scale for Vibe Palettes — scale_color_vibe
text: ggplot2 scale object.

title: vibeColors 0.0.1
text: Sequential palettes: Crimson, Plum, Azure, Turquoise, Emerald, Sunshine, Tangerine Diverging palettes: AzureCrimson, AzurePlum, TurquoiseTangerine, EmeraldTangerine, EmeraldCrimson Categorical palettes: CBsafe1, Avedon, Benedict vibe_palette(): Retrieve color palettes name optional reversal interpolation vibe_color(): Get specific colors name (e.g., “Azure100”, “Avedon1”) list_vibe_palettes(): List available palette names view_vibe_palettes(): Visualize palettes colored tiles scale_color_vibe() scale_fill_vibe(): Discrete color scales scale_color_vibe_c() scale_fill_vibe_c(): Continuous color scales Full support palette reversal reverse parameter Automatic color interpolation requesting colors palette contains
